message:
fix(core): preserve original inter-frame gaps in filtered replay sessions

The replay delay calculation used the timestamp of the immediately
preceding raw event (frame_index - 1), not the preceding *emitted*
event. When intermediate events were filtered out (e.g., by pane
filter), the visible replay timeline was compressed — a 2-second gap
between two visible frames would collapse to 500ms if filtered events
occupied the intervening timestamps.

Fix by tracking last_emitted_occurred_at_ms and computing delay from
the most recent frame the viewer actually saw. The first emitted frame
always gets Duration::ZERO regardless of its position in the raw event
stream. The field is reset on seek_to and reset operations.

Test: pane_filter_preserves_gap_between_emitted_frames verifies that
filtering pane 2's event at t=1500ms preserves the 2000ms gap between
pane 1's events at t=1000ms and t=3000ms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
